description: Evidence-based psychotherapy that identifies and changes negative thought patterns and behaviors
effectiveness: One of the most well-researched therapies; 50-60% response rate for depression

description: State of chronic workplace stress causing exhaustion, cynicism, and reduced efficacy
Mental health condition triggered by experiencing or witnessing a traumatic event
prevalence: About 20% of people exposed to trauma develop PTSD

description: Practice of intentional, non-judgmental present-moment awareness
research: Meta-analyses show moderate effect sizes for anxiety and depression reduction

description: Psychological model describing how early caregiver bonds shape adult relationships
clinical_relevance: Attachment style influences relationship patterns, therapy engagement, and mental health outcomes

description: Ability to recognize, understand, manage, and use emotions effectively
importance: Linked to leadership effectiveness, relationship quality, and mental health resilience

description: Pervasive pattern of grandiosity, need for admiration, and lack of empathy
prevalence: 1-6% of general population, higher in men
treatment: Long-term psychotherapy (psychodynamic, CBT); no approved medications
note: Distinct from healthy self-confidence; must cause significant impairment

description: Natural emotional response to loss; stages are non-linear and individual
models: Kübler-Ross 5 stages: denial, anger, bargaining, depression, acceptance
complicated_grief: Persistent complex bereavement disorder when grief is prolonged/disabling (>12 months)
timeline: No fixed timeline; most adapt within 1-2 years, though grief may resurface

description: Chronic brain disorder characterized by compulsive substance use despite harmful consequences
neuroscience: Hijacks dopamine reward circuitry; prefrontal cortex dysregulation impairs control

description: Bidirectional relationship: mental health disorders disrupt sleep; poor sleep worsens mental health
connections: REM sleep critical for emotional regulation

description: Intense fear of social situations due to fear of scrutiny, embarrassment, or humiliation
differentiation: Normal shyness vs disorder: does it significantly impair functioning?

description: Brain's ability to reorganize itself by forming new neural connections throughout life
clinical_relevance: Basis for why therapy and behavioral interventions work; recovery possible after brain injury
